comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Planned from execution-profile architecture review. Consensus: keep /mode as the user-facing command, keep /agents focused on enable/disable, model shared runtime through an execution layer (selector + prompt profile + execution policy + execution profile), and treat Pi as the runtime kernel while tau owns the control plane. Dependency order: tau-xipk -> tau-zrmw -> {tau-czbg, tau-btdv, tau-ba00} -> tau-91qi.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Ralph configuration scope refinement: `/ralph configure [optional-id]` is the canonical interactive UI for every Ralph state field classified configurable. Current expected configurable fields include active/available tools, enabled/available agents, maxIterations, itemsPerIteration, reflection cadence/instructions, execution profile recapture/view, and sandbox recapture/view. Runtime/system fields stay read-only or hidden.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Iteration 4 progress (Ralph loop tau-cux1):n- Fixed typecheck failures in test/ralph-config-service.test.ts (Option.none() for session fields, narrowed RalphConfigResult type).n- Implemented tau-t06i: /agents is now scope-aware and loop-state-backed in Ralph-owned sessions.n  - agents-menu/state.ts: preloadRalphOwnedSessionCache now caches loop metadata (loopName + enabledAgents from capabilityContract).n  - agents-menu/state.ts: isDisabledByRalphPolicy reads from loop metadata cache instead of project settings.n  - agents-menu/index.ts: accepts configureRalphAgents callback; Ralph-owned sessions mutate capabilityContract.agents.enabledNames via the shared config service.n  - app.ts: wires configureRalphAgents callback through runRalph -> configureLoopMany.n  - Normal sessions still edit project settings via AgentSelectionStore.n- Updated test/agents-settings.test.ts to reflect new loop-state-backed behavior (enabledAgents now comes from loop state, not project settings).n- npm run gate passes: 97 test files, 644 tests, 0 failures.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Handoff fix after completed Ralph config slice: `/ralph resume` was failing with stale ctx while applying the controller capability contract because active-tool application used the captured extension pi object after `ctx.switchSession`. Fixed by applying capability contract tools through the current command/replacement context. Also fixed review findings: negative Ralph ownership cache misses are no longer reused after child attachment, and config mutation safety now checks Option.isSome for active child sessions. Verification: `cd extensions/tau && npm run gate` passes (97 files, 646 tests). Remaining backlog still includes tau-mjud/tau-9de1 for the broader execution-control epic.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Follow-up Ralph smoke fix: `/ralph start/resume` could still fail in real Pi with `current session context cannot set active tools` after session replacement. Root cause: replacement command ctx does not always expose `setActiveTools`, while captured extension `pi` is stale after session replacement. Added session-file keyed capability-contract appliers registered from live session lifecycle events; replacement contexts with `setActiveTools` are preferred, appliers validate their captured session context before mutating Pi, stale appliers are unregistered in all flows, and child replacement sessions fail closed when no live applier exists. Verification: `cd extensions/tau && npm run gate` passes (97 files, 646 tests); review agent returned no P0-P2 findings.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Follow-up `/ralph configure` UI fix: replaced the previous ad hoc action handling with Pi TUI `SettingsList` submenus. Numeric fields now open input submenus, tools/agents use nested SettingsList toggles, execution/sandbox recapture use SelectList actions, and reflection instructions close the selector before opening Pi's editor. This avoids nested ctx.ui.custom calls while another custom component has focus and forwards input through the top-level component. Active-tool configuration filters system-managed `ralph_continue`/`ralph_finish` from both display and mutations. Verification: `cd extensions/tau && npm run gate` passes (97 files, 646 tests); review agent found no remaining P0-P2 issues.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Root-cause follow-up for the repeated `/ralph configure` redraw corruption: oracle confirmed this was not a selector API issue after the submenu rewrite. `SettingsList` row values must be single physical terminal lines, but the reflection-instructions preview used a raw slice of a multi-line string (`REFLECTION CHECKPOINTnnPause...`). Embedded newlines desynchronized Pi TUI's logical render lines from terminal physical rows, causing stale duplicated rows. Fixed by normalizing/truncating all SettingsList cell previews to single-line display text and stripping unsafe control characters; tool/agent descriptions are normalized before entering nested SettingsList submenus. Verification: `cd extensions/tau && npm run gate` passes (97 files, 646 tests).

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Follow-up `/ralph configure` mutability audit: sandbox profile was only exposed as a recapture submenu, so it was not directly switchable from the main settings view. Updated it to use SettingsList `values` so Enter/Space cycles `read-only`, `workspace-write`, and `full-access` from the top-level row while preserving approval timeout/subagent bits. Added a separate `Recapture sandbox` row for pinning current session overrides. Verified the other configure fields remain actionable: numeric fields use input submenus, tools/agents use toggle submenus, execution profile recaptures current session, and reflection instructions opens Pi's editor. Verification: `cd extensions/tau && npm run gate` passes (97 files, 646 tests); review agent returned no P0-P2 findings.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Implemented deferred Ralph configuration mutations for active child sessions. Non-scalar runtime changes (tools, agents, execution profile, sandbox profile) now queue in loop state while a child session is active and apply before the next iteration, including resume/no-pending paths. /ralph configure reports queued changes. /agents now preserves queued Ralph agent allowlist edits separately from current-session policy so queued changes do not leak into the active child. Verification: cd extensions/tau && npm run gate (passes; existing lint warnings only).
